reusable_components.description: EXIST in codebase. Can be reused.

items.name: load_config
items.what_it_does: Loads and parses a YAML configuration file from the specified path. Opens the file in read mode with UTF-8 encoding, uses yaml.safe_load() to parse the YAML content into a Python dictionary, and returns the parsed configuration dictionary.

items.name: get_printers_by_description
items.what_it_does: Filters and returns printers from the configuration that match the specified description/state. Enables data-driven testing by allowing test scripts to dynamically select printers based on their subscription state.

items.name: configure_logging
items.how_to_import: # This is a pytest fixture - automatically available in test files
items.what_it_does: Session-scoped pytest fixture that configures logging for the entire test session. Sets up both file and console logging handlers with INFO level and timestamp formatting.

items.name: config_loader
items.what_it_does: Configuration loader utility module providing functions to load YAML configuration files and filter printer configurations by description. Serves as the interface between the config.yaml file and test scripts.
items.helper_signature: module with load_config() and get_printers_by_description() functions

items.name: conftest
items.how_to_import: # Fixtures are automatically available in pytest test files
items.what_it_does: Pytest configuration file providing test fixtures, command-line options, and YAML test data loading functionality. Configures logging, defines custom pytest options for YAML-driven testing, and provides fixtures for accessing YAML test data.
items.helper_signature: pytest configuration module with fixtures and YAML test data loading

items.name: login.url
items.how_to_import:
from config_loader import load_config
config = load_config('config.yaml')
login_url = config['login']['url']

items.name: login.username
items.how_to_import:
from config_loader import load_config
config = load_config('config.yaml')
username = config['login']['username']

items.name: login.password
items.how_to_import:
from config_loader import load_config
config = load_config('config.yaml')
password = config['login']['password']

items.name: printers.pie_environment
items.how_to_import:
from config_loader import load_config
config = load_config('config.yaml')
printers = config['printers']['pie_environment']
items.constant_value: List of 5 printers with states: subscribed, out_of_trial_period, unsubscribed, initiate_unsubscribe, subscribed_no_pens

items.name: output.screenshot_dir
items.how_to_import:
from config_loader import load_config
config = load_config('config.yaml')
screenshot_dir = config['output']['screenshot_dir']

items.name: execute_click_action
items.what_it_does: Executes a click action on UI elements with metadata-driven locator resolution and iframe support. Loads page metadata, finds controls by text or label, handles iframe switching, and performs clicks with multiple fallback strategies.
items.method_signature: def execute_click_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: click_control
items.what_it_does: Clicks UI elements using progressive fallback through multiple locator strategies (XPath, CSS, ID, name, aria-label, data-testid, class, partial link text). Validates control metadata and attempts each strategy until one succeeds.
items.method_signature: def click_control(self, control, multiple, timeout: int = 10):

items.name: do_clicks
items.what_it_does: Core click execution method that waits for elements, validates their state (displayed and enabled), and performs clicks with optional delays between multiple clicks.
items.method_signature: def do_clicks(self, strategy, multiple, timeout, delay=0.10):

items.name: execute_vscroll_action
items.what_it_does: Executes vertical scroll actions either to a specific element (scrollIntoView with center alignment) or by page (PAGE_UP/PAGE_DOWN keys). Supports scrolling to elements identified by text using control metadata lookup.
items.method_signature: def execute_vscroll_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: execute_refresh_action
items.what_it_does: Executes browser page refresh by calling driver.refresh(). Logs the refresh action.
items.method_signature: def execute_refresh_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: execute_verify_action
items.what_it_does: Executes AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision models. Captures screenshot, optionally compares with reference image, sends to AI with detailed prompt, and returns structured JSON validation result. Supports variable substitution in prompts.
items.method_signature: def execute_verify_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: run_ai_task
items.what_it_does: Performs AI vision verification by sending screenshots to Azure OpenAI GPT-4 Vision model with detailed prompts. Supports optional reference image comparison. Base64 encodes images, constructs API messages, calls Azure OpenAI with specific parameters (max_tokens=6000, temperature=0, seed=12345 for determinism), and parses JSON response.

items.name: take_screenshot
items.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of the current browser page and saves it to the specified filename using driver.save_screenshot(). Returns the filename.

items.name: substitute_variables
items.what_it_does: Performs variable substitution in text strings, replacing ${VARIABLE_NAME} placeholders with actual values from the variables dictionary. Supports nested dictionary access (e.g., ${PRINTER.serial_number}). Uses regex pattern to find and replace all variable references.

items.name: set_variables
items.what_it_does: Sets multiple variables in the executor's variables dictionary for later substitution in test steps. Accepts keyword arguments and updates the variables dictionary. Logs each variable being set.

items.name: execute_step
items.what_it_does: Main step execution dispatcher that routes steps to appropriate action handlers based on action type. Validates step dictionary, extracts action and page, updates current_page, logs execution, dispatches to specific execute methods (click, set, verify, scroll, refresh, etc.), handles sleep delays with variable substitution.
items.method_signature: def execute_step(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: execute_flow
items.what_it_does: Executes a complete test flow from YAML data. Validates YAML structure, iterates through all steps, calls execute_step() for each, tracks timing and results, handles exceptions, generates CSV result report with step names, start times, durations, and status. Calls print_execution_summary() at the end.
items.method_signature: def execute_flow(self, yaml_data: Dict[str, Any], flow_result=None) -> None:

items.name: load_json_metadata
items.what_it_does: Loads UI element metadata from JSON files into the control_map. Parses page identifier (format 'module/page_name'), looks up folder name in metadata dictionary, constructs JSON file path, reads and parses JSON into control_map. Supports multiple modules (auth_controls, hpsmart, instantink, oss_emulator).

items.name: get_control
items.what_it_does: Searches for a UI control definition in the loaded control_map by matching the provided name against 'text' or 'label_text' fields of interactive elements. Returns the entire control dictionary containing all metadata (id, css_selectors, xpath_selectors, etc.) if found, None otherwise.

items.name: YamlStepExecutor
items.what_it_does: Core test execution engine that interprets and executes YAML-based test definitions. Loads JSON metadata files containing UI element definitions, manages test variables with substitution support, executes various action types (click, set, verify, navigate, scroll, etc.), handles iframe switching, performs AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision models, tracks execution logs, and generates CSV result reports.

items.name: MultiBrowserManager
items.what_it_does: Manages multiple browser instances with support for remote debugging, incognito mode, and normal browser sessions. Provides comprehensive window management including switching between browsers, finding windows by URL, waiting for new windows, bringing windows to foreground, and handling cross-platform window focus operations. Maintains a registry of all managed browsers with their metadata.

items.name: setup
items.what_it_does: Performs complete Playwright browser setup and returns a ready-to-use Page object. Configures browser type (chromium/firefox/webkit), viewport dimensions, locale, HAR logging, video recording, and proxy settings based on GlobalState configuration.

items.name: close
items.what_it_does: Performs complete cleanup of all Playwright resources including page, context, browser, and playwright instance. Verifies HAR and video files were saved if logging is enabled.

items.name: init
items.what_it_does: Initializes the framework configuration by resetting GlobalState and populating it with provided values and loaded data from locale_map.json, address_data.json, and printer_profiles.json. Sets up locale, language, region, country, printer profile, and other runtime configuration.

items.name: PlaywrightManager
items.what_it_does: Manages the complete lifecycle of Playwright browser automation including browser launch, context creation, page initialization, and cleanup. Implements context manager protocol for automatic resource management.

items.name: Config
items.what_it_does: Configuration initialization class that populates GlobalState from command-line arguments and loads supporting data from JSON files including locale information, address data, and printer profiles.

items.name: access
items.what_it_does: Navigates to the dashboard portal and handles login if needed. Waits for page load, navigates to portal shell URL, handles login if on login page, and verifies dashboard access by checking Instant Ink menu link visibility.

items.name: validate_context
items.what_it_does: Main validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Handles screenshot-only mode, full element validation, image comparison with exclusions, and animation validation.
items.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)

items.name: compare_images_detect_then_filter
items.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel image comparison with exclusion region filtering and bounding box generation for failures. Compares two images, filters out pixels in exclusion regions, generates suggested exclusion boxes for failures, and returns pass/fail status with detailed failure information.

items.name: save_full_page_screenshot
items.what_it_does: Captures a full-page screenshot and saves it with a timestamped filename. Creates output directory if needed, generates sanitized filename with timestamp, captures full scrollable page, and returns the filename.
items.method_signature: def save_full_page_screenshot(page: Page, flow_name: str, context_name: str, output_dir: str = None) -> str

items.name: take_element_screenshot
items.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of a specific element on the page identified by a selector and saves it to the specified path. Checks element visibility, creates output directory, captures element screenshot, and returns success status.
items.method_signature: def take_element_screenshot(page: Page, selector: str, output_path: str) -> bool

items.name: extract_text
items.what_it_does: Extracts the visible inner text from a Playwright element and returns it as a trimmed string. Gets user-visible text respecting CSS visibility, strips whitespace.

items.name: is_element_hidden_or_disabled
items.what_it_does: Checks if a Playwright element is hidden or disabled by evaluating JavaScript in the browser context. Checks offsetParent for hidden elements and disabled property for form elements.

items.name: get_element_position
items.what_it_does: Retrieves the position and dimensions of a Playwright element as a dictionary. Gets bounding box with x, y, width, height, returns dict with integer values, handles invisible elements gracefully.

items.name: extract_styles
items.what_it_does: Extracts computed CSS styles from a Playwright element by executing JavaScript in the browser context. Returns dict with font_family, font_size, font_weight, color, background, text_align properties.

items.name: get_current_timestamp
items.what_it_does: Generates and returns the current timestamp as a formatted string in the pattern YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM_SS. Uses datetime.now() and strftime for formatting.

items.name: sanitize_filename
items.what_it_does: Sanitizes a filename string by replacing all invalid filesystem characters with underscores. Uses regex to replace backslash, forward slash, asterisk, question mark, colon, double quote, less than, greater than, and pipe characters.

items.name: run_flow
items.what_it_does: Main orchestration function that executes a test flow in scrape, validate, or noop mode with comprehensive setup, execution, and reporting. Dynamically imports flow modules, sets up output directories, initializes configuration, executes flow with callbacks, handles exceptions, and finalizes reports.
items.method_signature: def run_flow(mode: str, flow_name: str, flow_args: Optional[Dict] = None, context_override: Optional[str] = None) -> None

items.name: DashboardHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive static helper class providing utility methods for all dashboard-related test operations including dashboard access, navigation, first-time setup, status card verification, plan management, billing operations, payment handling, printer selection, and various dashboard UI interactions.

items.name: CancellationPlanHelper
items.what_it_does: Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. Contains methods for selecting cancellation feedback, verifying cancellation UI elements, validating cancellation timeline, handling plan changes, and managing subscription restoration.

items.name: start_enrollment_and_sign_in
items.what_it_does: Starts enrollment and signs in the user with retry logic to handle timing issues. Navigates to enrollment URL, accepts privacy banner, and performs HPID authentication. Includes retry pattern that continues until user reaches printer selection page or timeout is reached.

items.name: select_printer
items.what_it_does: Selects a printer from the printer selection page by index and continues. Waits for printer selection page to load, clicks the radio button for the printer at specified index, and clicks continue button.

items.name: finish_enrollment
items.what_it_does: Completes the enrollment process by accepting terms and clicking through to completion. Handles TOS verification, checks terms agreement checkbox, clicks enroll button, and clicks continue on thank you page.

items.name: subscription_to_subscribed
items.what_it_does: Transitions a subscription to 'subscribed' state if not already in that state. Checks current state, accesses edit menu, selects 'event_hise_pens_inserted' event to trigger transition, saves changes, and verifies state is now 'subscribed'.

items.name: verify_rails_admin_info
items.what_it_does: Verifies that a Rails Admin field has the expected value with optional retry logic. Gets field text using RABaseHelper, asserts it equals expected value, and includes retry pattern with page reloads to handle async updates. Can wait up to 2 minutes for updates to propagate.

items.name: event_shift
items.what_it_does: Shifts subscription events forward in time by a specified number of days, optionally forcing billing cycle creation. Accesses edit menu, fills rollback field with days to shift, optionally enables billing cycle creation, saves changes, and verifies success. Commonly used to simulate time passing in tests without waiting for real time.

items.name: access_tenant_page
items.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific tenant's page in Gemini Rails Admin by searching for their email address. Navigates to fetch tenant URL, fills email with retry logic, submits search, clicks first result, and returns tenant ID.

items.name: sees_email_with_subject
items.what_it_does: Verifies that an email with a specific subject has been received by the specified email address within the timeout period. Polls Gmail API at regular intervals until email is found or timeout is reached. Raises RuntimeError if email not found within timeout.
items.method_signature: def sees_email_with_subject(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

items.name: EnrollmentHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper class providing static utility methods for automating HP Instant Ink enrollment flows. Contains over 100 methods covering all enrollment stages including terms acceptance, printer selection, plan selection, shipping address management, billing information, promotional codes, and enrollment completion. Supports multiple enrollment variants including standard web, OOBE, MOOBE, and FLIP flows.

items.name: GeminiRAHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper class providing static utility methods for interacting with the Gemini Rails Admin interface. Contains over 60 methods covering tenant management, subscription operations, billing cycle operations, payment credential management, printer operations, and extensive validation methods for verifying Rails Admin field values.

items.name: EmailHelper
items.what_it_does: Helper module for email-related operations in test automation. Provides utility methods for checking email receipt, extracting email content, and validating email subjects using Gmail API integration. Used for verifying email notifications sent during test flows.

items.name: FulfillmentServiceRAHelper
items.what_it_does: Helper module for interacting with the Fulfillment Service Rails Admin interface. Provides methods for accessing Rails Admin, navigating to orders by trigger ID, validating order status and shipping information, sending orders to fulfillment, and updating order error codes.

items.name: verify_previous_billing_cycle_period
items.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Clicks the select_1_option to select the first previous billing cycle. Uses expect().not_to_contain_text() to verify that the billing_cycle_period_title does not contain the text 'Current', confirming that a previous period was selected.

items.name: get_billing_cycle_option
items.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period by index from the dropdown. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Uses conditional logic to click the appropriate option based on period_index: if 1, clicks select_1_option; if 2, clicks select_2_option; if 3, clicks select_3_option. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with a 10-second timeout, confirming the table updated.

items.name: verify_billing_table_data
items.what_it_does: Verifies that the billing table displays the expected total pages printed. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Gets the inner text of the total_pages_printed element. Extracts numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts that the first extracted number matches the expected total_pages_printed parameter.

items.name: verify_correct_billing_section
items.what_it_does: Verifies all billing section fields against expected values from a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each field in the expected_values dictionary (total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total), performs validation by extracting numbers or validating currency values. This comprehensive method validates all billing section details in a single call.

items.name: validate_faq_on_print_and_payment_history
items.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ questions and links on the print and payment history page by clicking questions, verifying answers, and testing navigation links. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Tests all FAQ interactions and navigation flows.

items.name: see_notification_on_print_history
items.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden and expands it if needed. Gets the inner text of the print_history_table and asserts that the notifications_text parameter is contained in the table content.

items.name: verify_plan_info
items.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the print history page shows the expected price and page count. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible. Gets the text content of the plan_price_text element. Extracts numbers and asserts that the first number matches plan_value and the second matches plan_pages.
items.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

items.name: verify_current_total
items.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total value on the print history page matches the expected value. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible. Gets the text content of the first current_total_text element. Extracts numbers and asserts that the first number matches the expected total_value.

items.name: PrintHistoryHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations including verifying billing cycle periods, selecting billing cycle options, verifying billing table data, normalizing and validating currency values, verifying billing section details with expected values, validating all billing cycles data, validating FAQ links and navigation, validating no-pens print history page, downloading all invoices, verifying notifications, verifying plan information, and verifying current total values.

items.name: access_page
items.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking a navigation link with the provided page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the page_name parameter. The XPath pattern is '//a[@class="nav-link" and normalize-space(text())="{page_name}"]'. The normalize-space function removes leading and trailing whitespace from the link text for more reliable matching. Once the link is located, clicks it to navigate to the target page. This method is commonly used to navigate through the application's main navigation menu.

items.name: get_item_by_title
items.what_it_does: Locates and returns a card body element by searching for a card with a specific title in its header. Constructs a combined CSS selector that matches a div.card element containing either an h5.card-header with exact text matching the title or an h5.card-header with an anchor child whose text matches the title. The selector pattern is 'div.card:has(h5.card-header:text-is("{title}"), h5.card-header a:text-is("{title}"))'. First attempts to locate the card using page.locator(). If the card count is 0, waits up to 10 seconds for the card to appear. If still not found after waiting, raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating the field with that title was not found. If the card is found, returns a locator for the card body element within that card using card.locator('div.card-body'). This method is commonly used to extract data from labeled sections on detail pages.

items.name: get_field_text_by_title
items.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then calls inner_text() on the field element to extract all visible text content including nested elements. Finally strips leading and trailing whitespace using strip() and returns the cleaned text string. This method is commonly used to extract data values from labeled fields on detail pages for validation in test assertions.

items.name: wait_page_to_load
items.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with a dynamic selector constructed using an f-string that combines the page_title parameter into the selector pattern 'h1:has-text("Details for {page_title}")'. The method will wait up to the specified timeout duration (default 60 seconds) for the element to appear. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will throw a TimeoutError. This is commonly used after navigation to ensure the target page has fully loaded before proceeding with further interactions.

items.name: RABaseHelper
items.what_it_does: Provides a comprehensive set of static utility methods for interacting with Resque job scheduler, managing background job execution, navigating application pages and menus, extracting data from page elements by title, accessing links within titled sections, validating boolean field values, and validating template data with date/time comparisons. This helper class encapsulates common patterns for Resque job orchestration and page element interaction that are reused across multiple test scenarios. All methods are static so no instantiation is required.

items.name: click_print_history
items.what_it_does: Clicks the Print and Payment History menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the print and payment history page. Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with print_history_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the history page.

items.name: expand_instant_ink_menu
items.what_it_does: Expands the HP Instant Ink parent menu in the side navigation to reveal its submenu items. First checks if the overview_menu_link (a submenu item) is already visible. If not visible, it attempts to expand the mobile menu toggle if it is visible by clicking expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout with a 30000ms timeout, wrapped in a try-except block to handle cases where mobile menu is not present. Then clicks the instant_ink_menu_link to expand the parent menu. This method is useful when you need to verify submenu items are present without clicking them.

items.name: visible_instant_ink_menu_link
items.what_it_does: Waits for the HP Instant Ink menu link to become visible on the page with a very long timeout of 120000 milliseconds (2 minutes). Uses page.wait_for_selector with the instant_ink_menu_link selector string from self.elements and state='visible'. Returns the element locator once it becomes visible. This method is useful for ensuring the side menu has fully loaded before attempting to interact with menu items, particularly useful after page navigation or login.

items.name: faq_update_plan_link
items.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the update plan link within the FAQ section. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.update_plan_link selector to create a locator for the change plan link in the FAQ. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element.

items.name: plan_price_text
items.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the plan price description text element. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.plan_price_text selector to create a locator for the plan description element that displays plan pricing information. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element.

items.name: create_and_claim_virtual_printer_and_add_address
items.what_it_does: Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer plus adding shipping address. This is a high-level setup function that combines printer creation, claiming, and address setup into a single operation.

items.name: create_virtual_printer
items.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer through the G2 simulator API with retry logic for each step. Returns PrinterData tuple with entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint.

items.name: claim_virtual_printer
items.what_it_does: Claims a virtual printer to a tenant account through the UCDE API with retry logic. Associates the printer with the user's account so it can be enrolled in Instant Ink.
items.method_signature: def claim_virtual_printer(org_token, tenant_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, fingerprint)

items.name: add_address_to_tenant
items.what_it_does: Adds a shipping address to a tenant account via the Gemini comfe API. The address is required for subscription enrollment and cartridge shipments.

items.name: get_plans_data
items.what_it_does: Retrieves all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API for the current country. Returns array of plan objects with details like pages, pricing, plan IDs.

items.name: get_filtered_plan_data
items.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Filters the plans array to find plans matching the specified criteria.

items.name: subscription_data_from_gemini
items.what_it_does: Retrieves complete subscription data from Gemini API using service token. Returns full subscription details including state, free months, plan info, billing info.

items.name: get_subscription_state
items.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data. Returns the subscriptionState value or None if not found.

items.name: validate_subscription_state
items.what_it_does: Validates that subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds with polling. Asserts state equals expected_state.

items.name: send_rtp_devicestatus
items.what_it_does: Sends an RTP (Real-Time Printing) device status message to the G2 simulator for a virtual printer. Constructs complex binary telemetry data with page counts, supply levels, and printer state. This simulates printer reporting which triggers backend processing for page tracking and billing.

items.name: printer_colors
items.what_it_does: Returns the list of ink colors for the current printer based on supply variant. Returns ['CMY', 'K'] for IPH or ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] for IIC.

items.name: parse_flexible_date
items.what_it_does: Parses date strings in multiple formats into datetime objects. Handles various international date formats including US, European, and ISO formats.

items.name: extract_date_from_text
items.what_it_does: Extracts and parses a date from text containing mixed content. Uses regex patterns to find dates in various formats within text strings.

items.name: get_org_aware_token
items.what_it_does: Obtains an organization-aware user access token through a multi-step token exchange process. Returns tuple of (org_token, tenant_id).

items.name: get_token_gemini_client
items.what_it_does: Obtains an OAuth access token for the Gemini client using client credentials flow. Returns the access token string.

items.name: retry_operation
items.what_it_does: Generic retry wrapper that executes an operation with configurable retry logic. Provides automatic retry with delay and optional callback for cleanup/recovery.
items.method_signature: def retry_operation(operation, operation_name='Operation', max_attempts=5, delay=2, on_retry=None)

items.name: locale_map.json
items.what_it_does: Test data file containing comprehensive country and locale mapping configuration. Maps country names to ISO codes, supported languages, geographic regions (NA, EU, APJ), and card payment gateway assignments (PGS or 2CO). Supports 38 countries across three major regions.

items.name: printer_profiles.json
items.what_it_does: Test data file containing printer SKU profiles organized by printer model codename and geographic region. Each printer profile includes SKU numbers, easy enrollment capability flags, and supply variant types (IIC or IPH) for different regions.

items.name: offers.json
items.what_it_does: Test data file containing Instant Ink subscription offer configurations organized by country code. Each offer includes type (free or prepaid), unique GUID identifier, offer identifier code, payment requirement flag, and pricing in cents.

items.name: payment_method_data.json
items.what_it_does: Test data file containing payment method credentials and configuration for automated payment testing. Includes test credit card numbers for major card networks, IBAN details for direct debit, PayPal test account credentials, and Google Pay test credentials.

items.name: STACK_CONFIG
items.constant_value: Large nested dictionary with configuration for pie, stage, and production stacks

components_to_create.description: NOT in codebase. Must be created.

items.description: Go to Print and Payment History page under HP Instant Ink.
items.expected_result: Page loads successfully

items.description: Print some pages(less than the plan, such as 6 pages).
items.expected_result: Print job registered successfully

items.description: Refresh the dashboard page.
items.expected_result: Verify the "Complimentary pages" progress bar is filled with the black color and the value is displayed as correct (such as "6 of 10 used").

items.description: Continue to print some pages(more than the plan, such as 15 pages).
items.expected_result: Print job registered successfully

items.description: Refresh the dashboard page.
items.expected_result: Verify the progress bars for "Complimentary pages" and "Additional pages" are displayed well.

items.description: Check the "Additional pages" progress bar.
items.expected_result: Verify the progress bar is filled with the Yellow color and the value is displayed correctly(such as "5 of 10 used").

items.description: Check the icon.
items.expected_result: Verify the info icon is displayed well behind the string "Additional pages".

items.description: For Desktop: Move the mouse cursor on the info icon. For Mobile/Tablet: Click on the info icon.
items.expected_result: Verify a tooltip with correct message is displayed well.

items.description: Check the message below "Additional pages".
items.expected_result: Verify a message with the amount of blocks already bought is displayed well.

items.description: Check the Complimentary pages progress bar.
items.expected_result: Verify the "Complimentary pages" progress bar is full filled with the black color and the value is displayed correctly (such as "10 of 10 used").

items.description: Check total pages printed info.
items.expected_result: Verify correct number(such as 15 pages) displayed according to the printed pages.

items.description: Check the Billing Cycle Period card.
items.expected_result: Verify the plan pause information is not displayed.

items.description: Verify all UI elements are properly styled and responsive
items.expected_result: All elements display correctly on Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet

items.description: Even shift 32 days and trigger billing charge.
items.expected_result: Time shift and billing charge successful

items.description: Go to Print and Payment History page under HP Instant Ink.
items.expected_result: Page loads with updated data after time shift

items.description: Check the Billing Cycle Period card.
items.expected_result: Verify the plan pause information is displayed well.

items.description: Check the progress bar.
items.expected_result: Verify the Complimentary pages progress bar is displayed well.

items.description: For Desktop: Move the mouse cursor on the info icon. For Mobile/Tablet: Click on the info icon.
items.expected_result: Verify a tooltip with correct message is displayed well.

items.description: Check the Complimentary pages value.
items.expected_result: Verify the value is displayed as "0 of 10(Pause Plan) used".

items.description: Check the message below "Complimentary pages".
items.expected_result: Verify the information with correct plan info is displayed well.

items.description: Verify: Verify the plan pause information is not displayed.
items.expected_result: Verify the plan pause information is not displayed.
notes: No billing cycle card locators or verification methods found in batch 1. The billing_cycle_period_card and plan_pause_information_text elements are not present in any of the 5 JSON metadata files in this batch. These elements are specific to the Print and Payment History page and likely exist in a different batch containing that page's metadata.
No verification methods or locators for plan pause information found in this batch. Verification utilities for billing cycle card likely exist in page objects or helper files in other batches.
AI verification can validate element absence, but requires a well-crafted prompt. No traditional assertion methods for element visibility found in this batch.
Verifying that plan pause information is NOT displayed requires verification methods like is_element_visible() or verify_element_not_present(). Not found in this batch.

Found method to check element visibility state. However, the actual locator for plan pause information element is not in this batch. Page object with billing cycle card locators would be in a different batch.
